core_model.structure_summary: A continuous-time two-state Markov jump process alternating between two discrete configurations with constant transition rates, giving exponentially distributed dwell times in each state and a control-parameter-dependent bistability.

variables.meaning: a binary label indicating which of two discrete configurations the system currently occupies
continuous time with exponentially distributed residence intervals
the two constant transition rates, tuned by a scalar control (density) that drives the transition

deterministic_skeleton.summary: A first-order linear relaxation equation for the occupation probability of one state, with two constant rates, relaxing exponentially to a fixed point.

notes: The order parameters (milling M and polarization P) are observable definitions, not the core model. The central model is the two-state Markov process with exponential waiting times whose rates depend on a control parameter (confinement density), producing a continuous transition and bistability. Skeleton is the mean-field master/rate equation for the occupation probability, a linear first-order ODE.
